title: 10 best herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort are natural remedies derived from plants, providing relief from stomach cramps, nausea, and digestive issues. These oils help alleviate stomach discomfort by reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and soothing the stomach lining. The best herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort include Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Mentha x piperita, Cinchona officinalis, Cinnamomum verum, Curcuma longa, Cuminum cyminum, Peumus boldus, and Angelica archangelica. If left untreated, stomach discomfort can lead to missed workdays, social isolation, and a decline in overall well-being, severely impacting daily life and relationships.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with stomach discomfort because of its natural ability to calm digestive issues. The menthol content in peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the stomach, reducing cramps and spasms that can cause discomfort. It also has a cooling effect on the skin, which helps to ease bloating and gas. Additionally, peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, soothing irritation and discomfort. As a result, peppermint essential oils provide relief from indigestion, nausea, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Peppermint oil's ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract helps ease symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.
Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can alleviate heartburn, acid reflux, and ulcers.
Antispasmodic: Peppmint oil's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue helps calm spasms in the digestive tract, relieving symptoms of cramps, nausea, and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots are a popular choice for making essential oils, as they contain compounds that help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the gut.
Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant are rich in menthol and other active compounds that can help relax the muscles in the stomach and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a pesticide-free garden or store-bought organic source in the morning for maximum oil content.
Dry the freshly harvested peppermint leaves by spreading them thinly on paper towels for 7-10 days or using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours.
Crush the dried peppermint leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to increase their surface area for extraction.
Steep the crushed peppermint leaves in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a 1:3 ratio for 2-3 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the essential oil in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway essential oils helps with stomach discomfort because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe digestive issues, reducing symptoms of bloating, cramps, and indigestion. The oil's carvone content relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, alleviating spasms and cramping, while its antimicrobial properties help to reduce bacterial overgrowth that can contribute to stomach discomfort. Additionally, the calming scent of caraway essential oils can also ease stress and anxiety, which are often linked to stomach problems, promoting a sense of relaxation and overall digestive harmony.
remedy_properties: Digestive Spasmolytic: Caraway essential oil helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with indigestion, nausea, and vomiting.

Anti-Inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the gut lining, which can contribute to conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and gastritis, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, abdominal pain, and diarrhea.

Antioxidant: Caraway essential oil's antioxidant activity helps protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to stomach discomfort, ulcers, and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, caraway roots are valued for their ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Caraway seeds contain a unique blend of fatty acids and volatile oils that have been traditionally used to calm digestive upset and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality carum carvi seeds and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 30 seconds.
Dry the seeds in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth for 24-48 hours, changing the air occasionally.
Place the dried carum carvi seeds in a dark glass container with a lid and infuse them in a carrier oil like coconut or olive at a ratio of 1:5 for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, squeezing the solids gently to extract as much oil as possible, discarding the solids.
Store the final carum carvi essential oil in dark amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and use within 6 months, shaking the bottle well before each use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with stomach discomfort because it contains a compound called anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When ingested or applied topically, anethole helps to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and gas. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to expel trapped air and gas from the body, providing fast relief from stomach discomfort. Overall, fennel essential oils' unique blend of anti-inflammatory and carminative properties makes it a natural and effective remedy for alleviating digestive discomfort.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Fennel essential oil helps to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and gas by increasing the production of digestive enzymes and improving gut motility.
Carminative: The oil has carminative properties, which means it can help to prevent or alleviate flatulence and abdominal pain by reducing the formation of gas in the intestines.

Antispasmodic: Fennel essential oil exhibits antispasmodic properties, which help to relax and calm the smooth muscle contractions in the stomach, reducing symptoms of cramps, spasms, and diarrhea associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of fennel contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which are released during steam distillation and provide anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe stomach discomfort.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are a rich source of anethole, a natural compound with carminative and anti-spasmodic properties, making them effective in relieving digestive issues such as bloating and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers, ensuring at least one pound of plant material.
Dry the harvested plant material by air drying or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Crush the dried plant material into smaller pieces using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder to increase surface area.
Steam distill the crushed plant material in a still with 20% water at 100 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours, collecting essential oil condensate.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth, discarding solids and storing the clear liquid in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram essential oils helps with stomach discomfort because of its natural properties that soothe digestive issues. The oil has a calming effect on the muscles in the stomach, which can help alleviate cramps and spasms caused by indigestion or irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Additionally, marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, providing relief from bloating, gas, and abdominal pain. By promoting a healthy gut environment, sweet marjoram essential oils can help restore balance to the stomach and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Marjoram essential oil has carminative properties, which means it helps to expel gas from the digestive system and relieve bloating and cramps, promoting smoother digestion.

Anti-inflammatory: Marjoram essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).

Digestive tonic: Marjoram essential oil is a natural digestive stimulant that can improve digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and vomiting by increasing stomach contractions and promoting the release of digestive enzymes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing stomach inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum majorana leaves at dawn when they are most aromatic and potent.
Dry the harvested leaves in small batches for 3-4 hours using a food dehydrator or warm air circulation.
Transfer the dried leaves to a glass jar and infuse them in carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, before straining it through cheesecloth into another glass container.
Store the origanum majorana essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label it clearly with ingredients and preparation date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with stomach discomfort because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system. When ingested or applied topically, ginger oil can ease nausea and alleviate cramps associated with indigestion and bloating. Its warming properties also aid in stimulating digestion, reducing symptoms of IBS (Irritable Bowel Syndrome), and promoting a sense of relief from stomach discomfort. Additionally, its calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are often linked to stomach issues.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Herbal ginger essential oil helps stimulate digestive enzymes, improving digestion and relieving symptoms of indigestion and bloating. This is because it contains compounds like zingibain, which breaks down protein and increases gastric acid secretion.

Anti-Inflammatory Agent: The anti-inflammatory properties of herbal ginger essential oil help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), nausea, and stomach pain. This is due to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory eicosanoids and cytokines.

Antispasmodic Agent: Herbal ginger essential oil's antispasmodic properties help relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing muscle spasms and cramps that can cause stomach discomfort and pain. This is because it contains compounds like gingerol, which has a direct effect on the smooth muscle, reducing contractions and increasing relaxation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in volatile compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and digestive properties that help soothe stomach upset and nausea.
Roots: Containing a variety of bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger rhizomes by digging up roots from mature plants in the morning after dew has dried off.
Wash the harvested ginger rhizomes thoroughly under running cold water then scrub gently with a vegetable brush to remove dirt and debris.
Slice the cleaned ginger into thin pieces and place them in a clean glass jar with a lid leaving about one inch of headspace at top.
Add 25% of the jar volume with vodka or rubbing alcohol and seal the jar tightly then store it in a cool dark place for four weeks stirring daily.
After four weeks strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids then store the essential oil in an amber glass bottle in refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cumin essential oils helps with stomach discomfort because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The oil has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues such as indigestion, bloating, and cramps. Its carminative properties help to reduce gas and alleviate nausea, making it an effective remedy for relieving stomach discomfort. Additionally, cumin essential oils have antimicrobial properties that can help to combat the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy digestive system and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Herbal cumin essential oil helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause stomach discomfort.
Carminative property: The oil's carminative properties help to release trapped gas and reduce bloating, which can alleviate feelings of fullness and discomfort in the stomach.

Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal cumin essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other forms of stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cumin plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation in the stomach.
Barks: The barks of the cumin plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to relax the muscles in the stomach, alleviating symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of cuminum cyminum seeds and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the seeds in a single layer on paper towels or a clean cloth for 7 days.
Grind the dried seeds into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered seeds with 1 liter of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil at a ratio of 10% essential oil to 90% carrier oil and stir well for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a clean glass jar for 14 days, shaking the jar daily.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh satureja hortensis leaves at dawn on a sunny day with a clean pair of pruning scissors.
Wash the harvested leaves in cold running water for 30 minutes to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill the washed leaves in a clevenger apparatus using 10-15% water and 85-90% satureja hortensis material for 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distillation mixture and transfer it to a dark glass vial.
Store the satureja hortensis essential oil in a cool dry place at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze it for longer storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus serpyllum leaves in early morning after dew has dried ensuring maximum oil content.
Rinse collected leaves gently under cold running water to remove dirt and impurities immediately.
Steam-distill the leaves in a still or using a steam distillation apparatus for 30-45 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil from the distilled condensate through a paper filter or coffee filter within 2 hours.
Bottle the filtered oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in refrigerator at 40°f to maintain quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound essential oils helps with stomach discomfort because it contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marrubium vulgare leaves and flowers in early morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 3-4 hours or air-drying method.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a steam distillation apparatus with 10 liters of water and 100 grams of plant material.
Collect the essential oil from the condenser coil within 2 hours of completion of the distillation process.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pimpinella anisum leaves and stems from your garden or local market in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Wash the harvested plant material gently with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris and pat them dry with a clean towel.
Steam distill 500g of the cleaned plant material using a traditional still for 4-6 hours to extract the essential oils.
Collect the obtained essential oil in a sterile glass container and filter it through a coffee filter or cotton cloth to remove any impurities.
Store the final pimpinella anisum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid and keep it in a cool place away from direct sunlight for up to 12 months.
